title: Finding the absolute error
problem: A ruler measures to the nearest millimetre. A book is recorded as $185 \text{ mm}$ long. Find the absolute error of this measurement.

steps.instruction: Identify the precision of the instrument.
steps.working: The ruler measures to the nearest $1 \text{ mm}$, so the precision is $1 \text{ mm}$.

title: Calculating percentage error
problem: A thermometer is accurate to the nearest $2^\circ\text{C}$ and records a temperature of $30^\circ\text{C}$. Calculate the percentage error.

steps.instruction: Round to a suitable number of decimal places.

title: Comparing the precision of two instruments
problem: A measuring jug (accurate to the nearest $10 \text{ mL}$) records a volume as $200 \text{ mL}$. A graduated cylinder (accurate to the nearest $1 \text{ mL}$) records the same volume as $197 \text{ mL}$. Calculate the percentage error for each instrument and determine which is more precise.

steps.instruction: Find the absolute error for the measuring jug.
Find the absolute error for the graduated cylinder.
Calculate the percentage error for the measuring jug.
Calculate the percentage error for the graduated cylinder.
Compare the percentage errors and state which instrument is more precise.
steps.working: $0.25\% < 2.5\%$, so the graduated cylinder has the smaller percentage error and is therefore more precise.

answer: The graduated cylinder is more precise, with a percentage error of approximately $0.25\%$ compared with $2.5\%$ for the measuring jug.
